name: George Wettling
abstract: George Godfrey Wettling (November 28, 1907 – June 6, 1968) was an American jazz drummer. He was born in Topeka, Kansas, United States, and from  his early teens was living in Chicago, Illinois.

concerts.label: Paul Whiteman and His All American Band
Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon

about_quotes.speaker: John Beal
about_quotes.evidence: And I played with a lot of great drummers: ... George Wettling
about_quotes.doc_title: John Beal interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: George Wettling, the drummer, he heard me somewhere... he says come down to such and such a studio. I went there and it was Red Norvo and Mildred Bailey. And I sat in and first thing you know, I didn't know it was being recorded. We were making a record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 24, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: we were wonderful friends, and I lean towards many of his musical approaches as my own career continued. George and I first knew, after I followed him in Shaw's first big band... in 1936

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: The line-up was Jimmy McPartland, Bud Freeman, on tenor, and on piano we had -- who was playing drums -- George Wettling was playing drums, Dick Cary and we had Milt Hinton.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I had Muggsy Spanier and Mes Mesro and Baby Dodds and Pops Foster and George Wettling. In other words, I had enough to make up about eight bands.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: the band consisted of Wild Bill, Peanuts, Cutty Cutshall, George Wettling, Gene Schroeder and Arvell Shaw maybe or Walter Page
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001
